comment.body: Thanks for your answer! I did exactly as you explained, but it’s still not working. When saving or adding it to the chart, if I don’t put anything (leave it empty), the input section doesn’t appear in the settings later. If I fill in the input phrase when saving, the input section appears in the settings, but even after filling it in again, the lines still don’t show up on the chart. I do not understand, what is wrong?
comment.raw_body: Thanks for your answer! I did exactly as you explained, but it’s still not working. When saving or adding it to the chart, if I don’t put anything (leave it empty), the input section doesn’t appear in the settings later. If I fill in the input phrase when saving, the input section appears in the settings, but even after filling it in again, the lines still don’t show up on the chart. I do not understand, what is wrong?

comment.body:
The way I made it work in my case was:
1. Copy paste code in editor and save as new Indicator - IMPORTANT: When saving or adding to a chart it will ask you for the input, just skip that part/leave it empty
2. When the indicator is loaded in the chart, click on settings and in the input section copy paste the long string for the corresponding ticker.
3. It has been perfectly working for me since then, using it for SPX and ES
comment.raw_body:
The way I made it work in my case was:
1. Copy paste code in editor and save as new Indicator - IMPORTANT: When saving or adding to a chart it will ask you for the input, just skip that part/leave it empty
2. When the indicator is loaded in the chart, click on settings and in the input section copy paste the long string for the corresponding ticker.
3. It has been perfectly working for me since then, using it for SPX and ES

comment.body:
Alma, I was wondering if you would be interested in updating the Reversion Threshold script so that the levels are added where appropriate depending on which ticker you're seeing. I mean: If you're looking at SPX, SPX levels are shown. If you're looking at SPY, SPY levels are shown, if you're looking at ES, ES levels are shown.

This way, we could copy-paste all the levels you share into the pine editor each day and do not have to copy-paste for each ticker if we jump from one ticker to another.

I have a script that does this, so I could share it with you if you'd like to modify yours. If not, that's fine too :)

comment.body:
well, i was invited some of them, even big names to work with them, but as i always tell, its not my lifegoal, i said no to them. 
i only trust my own codes, not bcs i think they are the most accurate tools in the world, but bcs i know how they are calculated etc.

I only found one provider out-there who i would trust and this is OptionsDepth. 
we don't have partnership, i don't have link, or get money from them, nor i work for them, i'm just telling you.
SpotGamma also has some good tools (even tho I was a subscriper only 1 month long last year), but they are reliable too, but from greeks perspective I find OD the best. still under developping, but they will be fine.

So if you ask me ever again about providers etc. I could only parrot myself about this.

comment.body:
I know that in retail space you are probably fed by all this bullshit about naive gex profiles, zero gamma "negative gamma = bearish, positive gamma = bullish", 
support/resistance, etc. oversimplifications, but it is sadly way more complex than that.
this is why i want to build up the knowledge. If you paid, you probably trust me at some lvl, so be patient, and digest my educational posts i provided.
